title: Generator Start Failure
spl:
index=power sourcetype="generator:events"
| search start_result IN ("fail","abort") OR crank_count > 3
| table _time, generator_id, start_result, crank_count, fault_code
| sort -_time
description: Failed cranks or inability to reach rated speed leave the site on battery-only until resolved.
value: Failed cranks or inability to reach rated speed leave the site on battery-only until resolved.
implementation: Alert immediately on failed start during tests or utility loss. Track battery, starter, and fuel subsystem codes.
visualization: Table (start failures), Single value (failed starts 90d), Timeline (events).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Generator controller.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="generator:events"` (start_result, crank_count, fault_code).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Alert immediately on failed start during tests or utility loss. Track battery, starter, and fuel subsystem codes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="generator:events"
| search start_result IN ("fail","abort") OR crank_count > 3
| table _time, generator_id, start_result, crank_count, fault_code
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Generator Start Failure** — Failed cranks or inability to reach rated speed leave the site on battery-only until resolved.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="generator:events"` (start_result, crank_count, fault_code). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Generator controller. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: generator:events. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="generator:events". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Generator Start Failure**): table _time, generator_id, start_result, crank_count, fault_code
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (start failures), Single value (failed starts 90d), Timeline (events).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Power spikes during equipment power cycling, scheduled load shifting, or DR drills.
